steps.title: Identify the advantage signal
steps.content: The passage claims invasives are advantaged by delays in subfreezing temperatures. For a finding to directly support this, it must show the invasive species responding more favorably to the delayed temperatures than the native species does.

steps.title: Apply the advantage signal
steps.content: Choice C directly supports the conclusion by showing that P. aviculare extends its growing season much more than C. canadensis when subfreezing temperatures are delayed, proving the invasive capitalizes more on the extended conditions. The other choices either contradict this advantage, show no differential response to temperature change, or introduce a confounding variable. That matches choice C — C is correct.
